title: An old photo you keep, and the story behind it
summary: Tell the story behind one photo you still carry or keep at home.
topic: An old photo you keep, and the story behind it
prompt: Write 3 short paragraphs (150–200 words total) about one old photo you still keep. Use past simple for the story and present perfect for what has happened since. Include one detail you remember about the day the photo was taken.
hintStarters: I have kept the same photo…
I have moved house six times since…
sampleText:
I have kept the same photo in my wallet for almost twenty years. It shows my grandfather standing next to a small wooden boat he built himself. He was not a fisherman; he repaired bicycles for a living. But after his wife died, he started spending his weekends at the river. He told my mother that the water did not ask him questions.

I was eight when the photo was taken. I remember the smell of the wet wood and the way he laughed when the boat rocked. The boat sank during a storm two years later, and he never built another one. He died when I was fifteen.

The photo is now soft at the edges, and the colors have faded. I have moved house six times since then, and I have lost many things. But I have never put this picture in a drawer. Some objects ask you to keep carrying them.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the water did not ask him questions
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: dòng nước không hỏi ông câu nào

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple vs present perfect
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I was eight', 'he built himself', 'he died' kể câu chuyện trong quá khứ. 'I have kept', 'I have moved', 'I have lost' diễn tả những điều bắt đầu trong quá khứ và còn ảnh hưởng đến hiện tại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I was eight', 'he built himself', 'he died' tell the story in the past. 'I have kept', 'I have moved', 'I have lost' express things that started in the past and still matter now.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'for' + a length of time
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'for almost twenty years' đi với present perfect để chỉ khoảng thời gian kéo dài đến hiện tại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'for almost twenty years' goes with the present perfect to mean a period that lasts up to now.

sampleTranslationVi:
Tôi đã giữ cùng một tấm ảnh trong ví gần hai mươi năm rồi. Ảnh chụp ông tôi đứng cạnh một chiếc thuyền gỗ nhỏ mà ông tự đóng. Ông không phải ngư dân; ông sửa xe đạp để kiếm sống. Nhưng sau khi vợ ông mất, ông bắt đầu dành cuối tuần ở bờ sông. Ông nói với mẹ tôi rằng dòng nước không hỏi ông câu nào.

Tôi tám tuổi khi tấm ảnh được chụp. Tôi nhớ mùi của gỗ ướt và cách ông cười khi chiếc thuyền lắc lư. Chiếc thuyền chìm trong một cơn bão hai năm sau đó, và ông không bao giờ đóng chiếc nào khác. Ông mất khi tôi mười lăm tuổi.

Tấm ảnh giờ đã mềm ở mép, và màu sắc đã phai. Tôi đã chuyển nhà sáu lần kể từ đó, và tôi đã mất nhiều thứ. Nhưng tôi chưa bao giờ cất tấm ảnh ấy vào ngăn kéo. Có những vật yêu cầu bạn tiếp tục mang theo chúng.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the present perfect correctly with 'for'?
options: I keep the same photo in my wallet for almost twenty years.
I am keeping the same photo for almost twenty years.
I have kept the same photo in my wallet for almost twenty years.
I had kept the same photo for almost twenty years.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect ('have kept') + 'for + period' means the action started in the past and still continues.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense pairs with 'for + a length of time up to now'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses past simple inside the story of the photo.
options: I was eight when the photo was taken.
I am eight when the photo is taken.
I have been eight when the photo has been taken.
I was eight when the photo will be taken.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both clauses use the past simple because the event is finished and dated.
mcQuestions.hint: The story is in the past, so both verbs are in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most natural when describing a faded old photo?
options: The colors of the photo have faded over the years.
The colors of the photo are faded since many years.
The colors of the photo fades from a long time.
The colors of the photo had fade during years.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Have faded' = present perfect, used for a change that happened gradually and is true now.
mcQuestions.hint: Use present perfect for a change whose result you can see today.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which closing sentence stays on the topic of the photo, not a generic life lesson?
options: In conclusion, photos are very important in modern life.
Some objects ask you to keep carrying them.
Every person should take more photos every year.
Photos remind us that life is short and beautiful.
mcQuestions.explanation: Only B connects back to the specific image of carrying this particular photo; the others are generic platitudes.
mcQuestions.hint: Avoid the generic 'in conclusion' / 'every person should' ending.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there an object in your house that you would save first in a fire? What does that say about what matters to you?
